pages.raw_text:
At the meeting of the state fed
Grafton of women's clubs in Bell
ingham yesterday Mrs. H. W..
Allen of Spokane was reelected
president; Mrs. H. c. Blair of SpO-
kane, secretary, and Mrs. John
Trumbell of Port Angeles, treas
urer. Resolutions were adopted
asking the government to set aside
a forest reserve on Lake Quinalt
for a national park; favoring state
labor colonies; favoring a commits
sion to look after interests of I'M
employed; further investigation to
prevent spread of tuberculosis.


DR.. E. T. Mathes, president of
the Bellingham normal, in an ad-
dress declared co-education leaves
homes neglected, and said he hoped
to see the marriage laws changed
so that the prospective contractors
be made to show certificates of
proficiency in child raising before
a ceremony would be performed.
